parties.name: Social Democrats & Greens
parties.description: Focus on climate, welfare, and public services. Typical themes: green transition, healthcare, education.

parties.name: Liberals & Centrists
parties.description: Market-friendly with social safety nets. Often stress economy, EU integration, and civil rights.

parties.name: Conservatives & Right
parties.description: Emphasis on tradition, law and order, lower taxes, and national identity. Often stress security and borders.

skills.title: Your vote forces politicians to listen
Not voting = someone else decides for you

leaders.name: Dimitris Koutsoumpas
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 21. Marxist–Leninist party (est. 1918), the oldest political organization still active in Greece. Advocates workers’ rights, anti-imperialism, opposition to EU/NATO membership; socially conservative on some issues.

leaders.name: Sokratis Famellos
leaders.partyName: SYRIZA — Coalition of the Radical Left – Progressive Alliance
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 25 (reduced from original due to defections). Democratic socialist, progressive, pro-European party founded as a coalition of left-wing movements (2004). Governed Greece 2015–2019.

leaders.name: Alexis Charitsis
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 12. Pro-European party formed in late 2023 as a breakaway from SYRIZA. Emphasizes social equality, strong welfare state, education reform, and renewal of democracy; opposes war and genocide.
leaders.motto: Politics returns for the life we deserve

leaders.name: Nikos Androulakis
leaders.partyName: PASOK – Movement for Change (ΠΑΣΟΚ – Κίνημα Αλλαγής)
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 32. Social democratic party founded in 1974 by Andreas Papandreou. Pro-European integration; historically one of Greece’s two dominant parties. Currently the official parliamentary opposition.

leaders.name: Kyriakos Mitsotakis
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 156. Prime Minister. Liberal-conservative / Christian democratic, pro-European party founded in 1974 by Konstantinos Karamanlis. Advocates market-oriented economics, lower taxes, private enterprise, and EU/NATO integration.

leaders.name: Kyriakos Velopoulos
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 11. National conservative, right-wing populist party emphasizing strict immigration enforcement, national sovereignty, Greek Orthodox values, and a “Greece First” approach; mildly Eurosceptic.

leaders.name: Dimitrios Natsios
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 8. Founded in 2019. Emphasizes Orthodox Christian traditionalism and nationalism; traditional family values, national identity, and opposition to secularisation.

leaders.name: Zoe Konstantopoulou
leaders.partyName: Plefsi Eleftherias — Course of Freedom (Πλεύση Ελευθερίας)
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 5. Progressive, populist, anti-establishment party championing transparency, accountability, civil rights, and opposition to austerity policies.

leaders.name: Independent MPs
leaders.briefCv: Seats: 27. MPs who have left their original parliamentary groups and sit independently. No unified leader or platform.

quizzes.question: Before you choose a party, what is the most reliable first step to understand what they stand for?
options: Read the party’s programme/official website and compare it to their record and credible reporting
Only follow viral clips and memes because they’re short
Wait for a celebrity to say what to do
quizzes.explanation: Preparation starts with primary sources (official programme/website) plus independent reporting. Short clips can be misleading without context.

quizzes.question: Which approach is the fairest way to compare parties you disagree with?
options: Summarise their strongest argument in your own words before criticising it
Only read sources that already agree with you
Judge them only by a single quote taken out of context
quizzes.explanation: If you can accurately restate an opposing view, you’re less likely to vote based on misunderstandings.

quizzes.question: From the party cards on this site, which group explicitly says it opposes EU & NATO membership?
options: KKE — Communist Party of Greece
PASOK – Movement for Change
Nea Dimokratia — New Democracy
quizzes.explanation: In the leaders section, KKE’s description includes opposition to EU/NATO membership.

quizzes.question: Which leader on this page is identified as Prime Minister?
options: Kyriakos Mitsotakis
Nikos Androulakis
Zoe Konstantopoulou
quizzes.explanation: The Nea Dimokratia entry notes “Prime Minister” in the brief CV.

quizzes.question: Which signal is most useful to judge whether a political claim is trustworthy?
options: It cites evidence you can check (data, documents, official sources) and multiple credible outlets report the same facts
It makes you feel angry or thrilled, so it must be true
It has lots of likes and shares
quizzes.explanation: Virality and emotion aren’t evidence. Cross-checkable sources and independent corroboration are.

quizzes.question: If a party emphasises “strict immigration enforcement” and “national sovereignty”, which party card on this page matches that description?
options: Elliniki Lysi — Greek Solution
SYRIZA — Coalition of the Radical Left – Progressive Alliance
Nea Aristera — New Left
quizzes.explanation: The Greek Solution entry highlights strict immigration enforcement and national sovereignty.

quizzes.question: What’s the best way to handle a policy area you don’t understand (e.g., economy, migration, climate) before voting?
options: Pick 2–3 trusted explainers, learn the basics, then compare parties on that single issue using primary sources
Avoid it completely and decide only on vibes
Assume the loudest speaker is correct
quizzes.explanation: Being “prepared” doesn’t mean knowing everything — it means knowing enough to compare parties on what matters to you.

quizzes.question: On this page, which description fits PASOK – Movement for Change?
options: Centre-left, social democratic; pro-European integration
Far-right, religious conservative; opposition to secularisation
Right-wing populist; “Greece First”
quizzes.explanation: PASOK’s entry is described as social democratic and pro-European integration.
